title: Mumps - NHS
text:
n ambulance.
Bring any medicines you take with you.
Get vaccinated against mumps 
The MMRV and MMR vaccines can help prevent mumps. Both vaccines also protect you from measles and rubella , and the MMRV vaccine also protects you from chickenpox .
The MMRV vaccine is offered to all young children in the UK. The MMR vaccine is recommended for adults and older children if they were not vaccinated when they were younger.
Ask your GP surgery if you're not sure you or your child have had either of these vaccines. Your GP can vaccinate you for free on the NHS.
Find out more about the:
MMRV vaccine MMR vaccine
